title: Albion's Legacy - Quest for Glory! (A Top 10 Reevaluation)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I1sccj6fjQs

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns to Albion's Legacy for a thorough reevaluation, confirming it deserves its spot on his top 10 adventure games list despite real frustrations. The medium-sized box contains a staggering amount of content—character abilities, threat decks, hex tiles, relics, quests—and the deluxe version doubles down with additional scenarios, characters, and modular expansions. Yes, the rulebook has issues, and yes, those tiny named enemy tokens are a genuine storage nightmare. But here's what transcends those complaints: the game creates an unmistakable sense of place and time, surrounding Camelot with mountain wilderness to the north, forests to the east, wetlands to the west. That tangible exploration, combined with thematic depth rooted in Arthurian legend, feels genuinely epic.

Daniel appreciates how Albion's Legacy respects player agency through optional variants that let you tune difficulty, how each character quest creates narrative investment, and how the game avoids becoming merely luck-driven despite its randomness. Whether you're drawn to it for solo campaign play or group adventure, this is a game that understands both the mechanics and mythology of questing. Storage nightmares aside, Albion's Legacy remains a towering, underrated gem that rewards the effort to bring it to the table.

----

Have you played Albion's Legacy? Does the storage challenge deter you, or is the experience worth the organizational headache?
